headline: Oil Surges to $100, Sparking Fed Hike Fears; Trump Imposes New Tariffs
summary: Crude oil prices surged to $100/barrel, driving Treasury yields to 4.7% and increasing market expectations for a Federal Reserve rate hike. This macro shift triggered a tech sector sell-off, with Tesla and Alphabet plunging 14% and 7% respectively. Concurrently, the Trump administration announced new tariffs on over 80 nations, including a "forced labor tariff" impacting Korean supply chains.

bullets.lead: Crude oil prices hit $100/barrel, increasing Fed rate hike odds.

segments.text: The surge in crude oil prices to $100 per barrel for the first time since May has intensified concerns over inflation, leading to a rise in Treasury yields to 4.7% and increased market expectations for a Federal Reserve rate hike.

bullets.lead: Low layoff rates continue to defy AI job displacement fears.

segments.text: Despite ongoing discussions about AI's impact on employment, U.S. layoff rates remain low, indicating a resilient labor market.

sources.title: Low layoff rates defy AI jobs domination fears

segments.text: Many U.S. workers are reportedly staying in unwanted jobs primarily due to the necessity of maintaining health insurance benefits.

sources.title: U.S. workers are staying in unwanted jobs for health insurance

bullets.lead: Trump administration announced new tariffs on over 80 nations.

segments.text: The Trump administration is set to impose new tariffs of approximately 10% on goods from more than 80 global trading partners, signaling a significant shift in trade policy.

sources.title: Trump Administration to Impose New Tariffs of Around 10% on Over 80 Nations

segments.text: This move includes a 'forced labor tariff' that could impose a 12.5% burden, particularly impacting Korean supply chains and necessitating a re-evaluation of manufacturing strategies.

segments.text: Following the announcement of new tariffs by the Trump administration, Canada is reportedly considering retaliatory measures to protect its economic interests.

sources.title: Canada to consider retaliating against latest Trump tariffs, Carney says

bullets.lead: EU fined Google $1 billion amid escalating trade tensions.

segments.text: Europe's antitrust regulators fined Google $1 billion for its search engine practices, a decision that came just before the expected announcement of new U.S. tariffs, highlighting ongoing international trade and regulatory frictions.

sources.title: Europe just fined Google $1 billion, the day before Trump is expected to announce tariffs

bullets.lead: AMD secured a significant AI GPU deal and investment with Anthropic.

segments.text: AMD announced a strategic partnership with Anthropic, including a $5 billion equity investment and a deal for Anthropic to purchase up to 2 gigawatts of AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs.

sources.title: 《科技》AMD再攻AI基建！Anthropic導入2GW MI450、加碼50億美元投資- 產業

bullets.lead: Nvidia is expanding its chip packaging capabilities and HVDC production.

segments.text: Nvidia and Amkor have entered a $1.5 billion chip packaging deal, enhancing the supply chain for advanced AI chips.

sources.title: Nvidia, Amkor strike $1.5 billion chip packaging deal

segments.text: Nvidia's 800V HVDC is set to enter production in Q1 2027, with Delta Electronics ramping up production in Q2 2027, supporting future AI infrastructure.

sources.title: Nvidia 800V HVDC enters production in 1Q27, Delta set for 2Q ramp

bullets.lead: U.S. Commerce Department is investigating Moonshot for restricted chip access.

segments.text: The U.S. Commerce Department is investigating Moonshot for allegedly accessing restricted Nvidia chips and distilling Anthropic's Fable model to train Kimi K3.

bullets.lead: Google significantly raised its FY26 capex guidance for cloud and AI.

segments.text: Google increased its FY26 capital expenditure guidance to $195B-$205B from $180B-$190B, with significant increases expected in 2027, driven by accelerating Google Cloud revenue and Gemini AI demand.

bullets.lead: OpenAI and SpaceX are investing in large-scale data center infrastructure.

segments.text: OpenAI is set to provide up to 1,000 MW of flexible power demand to Georgia, indicating the massive energy requirements for its AI operations.
SpaceX is reportedly laying groundwork to build at least one new large-scale data center in Texas, signaling its expansion into significant AI infrastructure.

bullets.lead: GE Vernova secured over $5 billion in data center electrification orders.

segments.text: GE Vernova reported strong electrification revenue and over $5 billion in data center orders year-to-date, more than double its 2025 total, highlighting surging demand for power in data centers.

bullets.lead: Alphabet's stock plunged amid concerns over rising AI spending.

segments.text: Alphabet shares, along with Tesla, plunged as investor concerns over escalating AI spending and its impact on cash burn rattled the market.

segments.text: Intel reported $16.1 billion in revenue, marking its fastest growth in 15 years, primarily fueled by robust demand for AI-related semiconductors.

x_themes.topic: Google Raises FY26 Capex Guidance to $195B-$205B, Driven by Accelerating Cloud & Gemini AI Demand
x_themes.summary: Google increased its FY26 capex guidance to $195B-$205B (from $180B-$190B) and expects significant increases in 2027, driven by accelerating Google Cloud revenue (up 82% YoY), 950M monthly Gemini users, and strong AI compute demand that exceeds supply. This signals robust, sustained investment in AI infrastructure.

x_themes.topic: AMD Invests $5B in Anthropic, Secures 2 GW MI450 GPU Deal
x_themes.summary: AMD announced a strategic partnership with Anthropic, including a $5 billion equity investment and a deal for Anthropic to purchase up to 2 gigawatts of AMD Instinct MI450 Series GPUs starting H1 2027. The collaboration also involves engineering efforts to optimize AI workloads and ROCm software development.

x_themes.topic: ServiceNow Defies AI Replacement Fears with Strong Q2 Growth, AI Monetization Still Early
x_themes.summary: ServiceNow reported strong Q2 results with accelerating subscription revenue growth and expanding operating margins, indicating robust demand and defying concerns about AI replacing its services. While AI features are driving discussions, actual adoption and monetization of "Now Assist" remain early, with some customers resisting higher pricing for AI features.

x_themes.topic: Open-Source AI's Impact on Memory Demand & Tiering
x_themes.summary: Discussion suggests open-source AI models could multiply global memory demand, as distributed deployments and large KV caches for long-context tasks require replicated HBM pools and memory tiering across HBM, DRAM, and NAND, rather than concentrating demand in a few datacenters.

x_themes.topic: Nanya Technology's New EUV Fab and Advanced Memory Development
x_themes.summary: Nanya Technology is set to open a new NT$500 billion Taishan fab using EUV lithography, while also developing custom AI Ultra-Wide-I/O memory chips for Edge AI and Wafer-on-Wafer (WoW) production technology.

x_themes.topic: Datacenter Power Demand: OpenAI to Provide Flexible Demand to Georgia
x_themes.summary: OpenAI is set to provide up to 1,000 MW of flexible power demand to Georgia, indicating the massive energy requirements of its AI operations and potential grid integration strategies.

x_themes.topic: Datacenter Power Demand: GE Vernova Secures Over $5B in Datacenter Electrification Orders
x_themes.summary: GE Vernova reported strong electrification revenue and over $5 billion in data center orders year-to-date, more than double its 2025 total, highlighting surging demand for power infrastructure to support data center expansion.

x_themes.topic: Datacenter Power Demand: SpaceX Building Large-Scale Data Center in Texas
x_themes.summary: SpaceX is reportedly laying groundwork to build at least one new large-scale data center in Texas, signaling its expansion into significant AI infrastructure.

x_themes.topic: US Investigates Moonshot for Accessing Restricted Nvidia Chips and Distilling Anthropic Model
x_themes.summary: The U.S. Commerce Department is investigating Moonshot for allegedly accessing restricted Nvidia chips and distilling Anthropic's Fable model to train Kimi K3, potentially leading to Moonshot's addition to the Entity List and restricting access to U.S. technology.

x_themes.topic: 3M's Expanded Beam Optical Connectivity Certified by Microsoft
x_themes.summary: 3M developed expanded beam optical connectivity technology, certified by Microsoft for Azure data centers, which accelerates fiber-optic network deployment for hyperscalers amid the AI boom. This technology is projected to generate $40-50M in 2024 revenue, potentially scaling to $200-250M.

x_themes.topic: Soitec's Photonics-SOI Sales Growth & Capacity Expansion
x_themes.summary: Soitec reported strong Q1 FY27 revenue, with Photonics-SOI sales nearly doubling year-over-year, and expects FY27 Photonics-SOI revenue to more than double. The company has qualified Singapore for high-volume production and secured multi-year capacity commitments.

x_themes.topic: Credo (CRDO) Growth in AECs and Optical Portfolio
x_themes.summary: Credo is seeing significant growth in active electrical cables (AECs) and its optical portfolio, which includes DSPs, silicon photonics, and complete modules, with management expecting over $500M from optics in FY27. The company maintains strong operating margins despite revenue nearly doubling.

x_themes.topic: Potential Bottleneck in CW Laser Supply for Photonics
x_themes.summary: A potential bottleneck is emerging in the supply of Continuous Wave (CW) lasers, crucial for photonics, due to increasing demand from multiple architectures ramping simultaneously in 2027/2028. This could impact key optical component suppliers.

x_themes.topic: Sivers Semiconductors (SIVE) Insider Buys Amid Drawdown
x_themes.summary: Sivers Semiconductors (SIVE) saw significant insider buying from its board and CEO, with purchases made before lock-up expiry, indicating strong confidence in the company's prospects despite a recent stock drawdown.
